checks.failText: Using {cpu.percent}% CPU with {sample.hotConcentration|pct} of it in {sample.hotFunction} ({sample.hotBinary}) — a tight loop or heavy computation.
checks.passText: CPU work is spread out — not stuck in a loop.
checks.title: Stuck in a loop

checks.failText: Averaging ~{cpu.sustainedPercent}% CPU over the last hour — holding this much CPU for so long usually means a stuck task, a busy loop, or runaway background work, even though it stays below the instantaneous-spike level.
checks.passText: No sustained high CPU over the recent window.
checks.title: Sustained high CPU

checks.failText: The main thread is blocked in {sample.mainFunction}, not its run loop — the app is frozen.
checks.passText: The main thread is servicing its run loop — responsive.
checks.title: Not responding

checks.failText: Memory is climbing steadily (~{memory.leakSlopePerMin|bytes}/min, {memory.leakConfidence|pct} confident over {process.uptime}) — a consistent upward trend, the signature of a leak.
checks.passText: No consistent upward memory trend — no sign of a leak.
checks.title: Possible memory leak

checks.failText: Memory is rising, but the process has only been running {process.uptime} — likely normal start-up growth; re-check once it has been up a while.
checks.title: Memory still warming up

checks.failText: Reading {disk.readRate|rate} from disk — heavy read activity.
checks.title: High disk reads

checks.failText: Writing {disk.writeRate|rate} to disk — heavy write activity.
checks.title: High disk writes

checks.failText: Open file descriptors are climbing ({fd.count} now) — they may be leaking (opened but not closed).
checks.title: Possible descriptor leak

checks.failText: {socket.count} open connections — unusually many (a leak or storm).
checks.title: Network connections
